Shawna Thomas
Email: sthomas@tamu.edu

Office Hours
I would be happy to meet with you. Email me to set up a time that works for both of us.
Preferred Contact Method

chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content: High-Impact Experience. (0-0). Credit 0. Participation in an approved high-impact learning practice; documentation and self-assessment of learning experience. Prerequisite: Junior or senior classification; also taught at Galveston campus.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): Junior or senior classification; also taught at Galveston campus.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, you will be able to:
• Identify key features of your high-impact learning experience
• Reflect on how your high-impact learning experience impacted you

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
This material Is: Optional
No Textbook Needed

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
This course is graded on a pass/fail (S/U) basis.
To pass (S), you must:
• have an approved high-impact learning experience completed and
• submit an acceptable self-reflection with acceptable external documentation by the last day of classes.
The high-impact learning experience must meet the Department of Computer Science and Engineering's high-impact experience policy (included in the course materials). The instructor will decide if a submission is “successful" or "incomplete.” Incomplete submissions will be returned to the student for revision and must be resubmitted by the last day of classes.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content: There is no penalty for late submissions, however submissions and resubmissions will not be accepted after the last day of classes.

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
Course assignment dates:
1. Activity Checker Survey [due end of week 1, closes last day of classes]: verify your completed activity qualifies
2. ENGR[x] Submission (required to pass) [due end of week 11, closes last day of classes]: describe your activity, provide external documentation of successful completion, and reflect on your experience
• Your external documentation must be from the sponsor of the experience and confirm successful completion. (Offer letters for the activity do not qualify.)
• Your self-reflection must follow the format provided on Canvas.
Submissions and resubmissions will not be accepted after the last day of classes.

chunks.title: Additional Course Information
chunks.content: The materials used within this course are copyrighted. These materials include, but are not limited to, the syllabus, quizzes, assignments, activity descriptions, online content, course videos, audio and visual recordings of classes, etc. Because these materials are copyrighted, you do not have the right to copy or distribute these materials, unless permission is expressly granted.

chunks.title: AI Statement
chunks.content: According to the Texas A&M University Definitions of Academic Misconduct, plagiarism is the appropriation of another person's ideas, processes, results or words without giving appropriate credit (aggiehonor.tamu.edu). You should credit your use of anyone else's words, graphic images, or ideas using standard citation styles. Artificial Intelligence (Al) text generators and natural language processing tools (colloquially, chatbots - such as ChatGPT), audio, computer code, video, and image generators should not be used for any work for this class without explicit permission of the instructor and appropriate attribution. This includes, but is not limited to, 1. Creating or revising drafts 2. Editing your work 3. Reviewing a peer's work This excludes pre-existing software additions such as spelling and grammar checkers, which are acceptable.

chunks.title: Statement on Mental Health and Wellness
chunks.content: Texas A&M University recognizes that mental health and wellness are critical factors influencing a student's academic success and overall wellbeing. Students are encouraged to engage in healthy self-care practices by utilizing the resources and services available through University Health Services. The TELUS Health Student Support app provides access to professional counseling in multiple languages anytime, anywhere by phone or chat, and the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline offers 24-hour emergency support at 988 or 988lifeline.org. Texas A&M College Station Students needing a listening ear can contact University Health Services at 979.458.4584. Call 911 or visit your nearest emergency room if you are currently experiencing a life-threatening situation or if your safety is at risk. 24-hour emergency help is also available through the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (988) or at 988lifeline.org.
